title: Windows first-run UX and diagnostics hardening
acceptanceCriteria: Runtime adapters use a cross-platform Codex harness and package tests pass on Windows/macOS/Linux without POSIX-only test scripts.
Bootstrap reports missing config and Codex smoke warnings as warn, uses PATH-based Git detection, and preserves machine-readable report JSON.
User-facing first-run messaging is concise and actionable for missing Codex CLI, unpaired host, and occupied miniapp port; verbose details stay in doctor/JSON diagnostics.
CLI bootstrap output is structured and compact, and at least one user-facing progress indicator is shown without changing existing APIs unnecessarily.
Miniapp handles port conflicts without an unhandled stack trace and host-daemon suppresses repeated expected first-run noise.
Tests cover the cross-platform runtime harness, bootstrap warning expectations, diagnostics/text rendering split, and reduced-noise startup or miniapp port-conflict handling.
